mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE jumps > 0.3pp, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that forces corporate margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline × HOUST decline) shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX returns do not differ significantly between high/low interaction quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime, sustained elevated CPI readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which increases the cost of capital and suppresses equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS show no negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, or the signal flips to positive during periods of sticky inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, driving positive momentum in equity prices over the immediate 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no positive correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns, or the signal generates negative forward returns in more than 50% of positive GDP growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding falls below the 650,000 million dollar threshold, it signals a credit crunch that restricts household spending and corporate financing, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where RSAFS drops below 650,000 million, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive power over baseline SPX momentum.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls growth and consumer credit expansion reflects a wage-price spiral that initially boosts equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and RSAFS acceleration shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window, or SPX 5-day returns are not significantly higher when both series accelerate simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained environment where industrial production outpaces housing construction, inventory buildups force corporate price cuts and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO to HOUST ratios show no negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns during supply bottlenecks, or the signal fails to predict returns when industrial capacity utilization exceeds 80%.
